sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
ascii_grid = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
# Find orange/blue platform
platform_positions = []
for r in range(64):
    for c in range(64):
        if ascii_grid[r][c] in 'Ob' and 10 < r < 55:
            platform_positions.append((r, c))
print(f"Platform positions: {platform_positions}")
if platform_positions:
    rows = [p[0] for p in platform_positions]
    cols = [p[1] for p in platform_positions]
    print(f"Platform: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# Check target position
print("\nTarget (blue at top):")
for r in range(10, 15):
    for c in range(33, 40):
        if ascii_grid[r][c] == 'b':
            print(f"  ({r},{c}) = 'b'")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Interesting! The platform seems to have merged with the target! The platform is now at rows 11-29, cols 34-38. But wait, the target is at rows 11-13, cols 35-37, and the platform's top part is also at rows 11-13, cols 35-37. They overlap!

- After 2nd UP: rows 30-34
- After 3rd UP: rows 25-29
So the platform should be at rows 25-29, cols 34-38. But the output shows positions at rows 11-13 as well, which are the target pixels.

I need to continue moving UP. The platform needs to go from rows 25-29 to rows 10-14 (or similar) to overlap with the target at rows 11-13.
From rows 25-29, I need to move UP by 15 cells (3 more UP actions) to reach rows 10-14.
But wait, the corridor is at rows 17-24. The platform is currently at rows 25-29, which is below the corridor. To enter the corridor, I need to move UP. The corridor is at rows 17-24, cols 33-37. The platform is at cols 34-38, which partially overlaps with the corridor.
Let me try moving UP 3 more times to see if the platform can pass through the corridor and reach the target.
